title: Tree utility functions for PseudoFileTree component
purpose: Provides utilities for building, sorting, and filtering hierarchical file tree structures from flat file lists.

steps.content: Check if the node has a children map property.
If the node has a children map, convert its values to an array of child nodes.
Delete the temporary children map from the node after conversion.
Return the node with converted children.
Initialize an empty object to hold root nodes.
Process each file path in the input list.
Split each file path into its component parts.
Traverse the path parts to build the tree structure.
Create nodes for each path part as needed.
Ensure each node has a children array.
Convert directory nodes to use a children map for efficient access.
Convert the root object to an array and recursively convert all children maps.
Create a copy of the input nodes array.
Sort the nodes by directory status and then alphabetically.
Place directories before files in the sorted result.
Sort the top-level nodes using the sorting function.
Recursively sort the children of each node.
Return the sorted tree structure with all levels properly ordered.

methods.name: filterTree
methods.normalized_params: (nodes: TreeNode[], query: string, ancestorMatched: boolean): { nodes: TreeNode[]; expandedPaths: Set<string> }

steps.content: Initialize an empty set to track expanded paths.
Process each node in the input list.
Check if the node name matches the query string.
Recursively filter the children of the node if they exist.
Determine if the node should be included in the result.
IF the node should be included, add its path to the expanded paths set.
Include the filtered children in the returned node.
Return the filtered nodes and the set of expanded paths.
